  "transcript": [
    "> hi kiro, can you help me create a website for my girl that will have a sweet theme and list our interests and bucket lists with a background music that i can import to",
    "\n\nThat sounds like a really sweet project! I'll create a cute, themed website for you and your girl with:\n\n- A sweet/romantic theme with soft colors\n- Sections for shared interests and bucket list\n- Background music support (you just drop in your own audio file)\n\nLet me build it:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Your site is at `~/Downloads/love-site/index.html`. Here's what you get:\n\n🌸 Sweet pink/rose gradient theme with floating heart animations\n💕 \"Our Interests\" section with cute pill-shaped tags\n🪣 \"Our Bucket List\" section with heart bullet points\n🎵 A music button (bottom-right corner) to play/pause background audio\n✨ Glassmorphism card design with Google Fonts (Dancing Script + Quicksand)\n\nTo get it working:\n\n1. Drop your music file into the `love-site` folder and rename it to `music.mp3` (or edit the `<source src=\"music.mp3\">` line in the HTML to match your filename)\n2. Open `index.html` in your browser to preview\n3. Edit the interests and bucket list items directly — I left `✏️ EDIT` comments to mark those spots\n\nYou can personalize the header text, add photos, or change any of the placeholder interests/bucket list items to match you two. Want me to tweak anything — colors, layout, add a photo gallery, or a timeline section?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> can you make the you & me into Kirsty and Jet. then make the theme like skyblue our favorite color",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "All updated! Here's what changed:\n\n💙 Header now says \"Kirsty & Jet\"\n🩵 Entire theme switched to sky blue — gradient background, tags, buttons, accents, everything\n🫧 Floating emojis are now blue hearts, diamonds, bubbles, and clouds instead of pink hearts\n\nGo ahead and refresh `index.html` in your browser to see the new look. Want me to adjust anything else — maybe add a photo section or a special message area?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> can you make the mouse when cursoring there is an effect",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Let me read the current file to find the right spot to insert the cursor effect:\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> yy",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Now when you move your mouse around the page, little sparkles will trail behind the cursor — blue hearts 💙, stars ⭐, sparkles ✨, and light blue hearts 🩵 that fade out as they float up.\n\nRefresh the page and give it a try! Want anything else added?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> is this site can be viewed also on mobile perspective also like responsive design",
    "\n\nThe site already has the viewport meta tag and uses relative units (`rem`, `%`, `max-width`) so it's mostly responsive — but it could use a few tweaks for smaller screens. Let me add that:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "All set! Here's what the site already had going for it plus what I just added:\n\nAlready responsive:\n- `<meta name=\"viewport\">` tag ✅\n- `max-width: 800px` container that centers on large screens ✅\n- `rem` and `%` based sizing ✅\n- Flexbox wrapping on the interest tags ✅\n\nJust added:\n- Media query for screens under 600px — scales down the heading, section padding, tags, and spacing so it looks clean on phones\n\nIt'll look good on mobile now. Want me to open it so you can check, or add anything else?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> yes",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "Opened it in your browser! You can test the mobile view by right-clicking → \"Inspect\" → clicking the device toggle icon (or `Cmd + Shift + M` in Chrome/Firefox) to simulate different phone screens. Let me know if you want any tweaks!\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> for the bucketlist is it possible that we can add activity or event and also we can fill the heart if it is already been done",
    "\n\nLove that idea! I'll make the bucket list interactive — you can add new items and click the heart to mark them as done. It'll save your progress too using localStorage so it persists across refreshes.\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now replace the bucket list HTML and add the interactive script:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Here's what the bucket list can do now:\n\n🤍 → ❤️ Click the heart to mark an item as done (it fills up and the text gets a strikethrough)\n❤️ → 🤍 Click again to unmark it\n➕ Type a new activity in the input box and hit \"Add 💙\" or press Enter\n💾 Everything saves to localStorage — so your progress stays even after closing the browser\n\nRefresh the page and try it out! Want anything else?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> for the our interests when hovering to the categories can we have an effect also",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Now when you hover over each interest tag, it'll:\n\n- Scale up slightly and float upward\n- Get a blue glow shadow underneath\n- Shift to a deeper blue gradient\n\nSmooth transitions in and out. Refresh and try hovering over them! Want anything else added?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> how can i import a music",
    "\n\nJust drop your music file into the `love-site` folder and rename it to `music.mp3`. That's it!\n\nYour folder should look like:\n```\n~/Downloads/love-site/\n├── index.html\n└── music.mp3   ← your song here\n```\n\nThen click the 🎵 button on the bottom-right of the page to play it.\n\nIf your file has a different name or format, you have two options:\n\n1. Rename it to `music.mp3`\n2. Or edit the source line in `index.html` to match your filename, for example:\n```html\n<source src=\"your-song-name.mp3\" type=\"audio/mpeg\">\n```\n\nSupported formats: `.mp3`, `.ogg`, `.wav` — mp3 works best across all browsers.\n\nLet me know if you need help with anything else!\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> on the bucketlist can we also edit or discard an activity?",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",

    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Each bucket list item now has two extra buttons:\n\n- ✏️ Edit — pops up a prompt to change the text\n- 🗑️ Delete — asks for confirmation then removes it\n\nThey appear slightly faded and become fully visible on hover so they don't clutter the design. Everything still saves to localStorage. Refresh and try it out!\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> can we add also modification of the theme into palette colors so we can change it to the color of the theme we want",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "I'll convert all the colors to CSS variables and add a palette picker. This way switching themes is instant.\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> yy",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "Now I'll rewrite the whole file with CSS variables and a palette picker:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",

    "> y",
    "Now add the palette button and panel HTML:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now add the theme switcher script before the closing `</script>`:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! You now have a 🎨 palette button on the bottom-left corner. Click it and you'll see 6 theme options:\n\n- 🩵 Sky Blue (your current default)\n- 🩷 Rose Pink\n- 💜 Lavender\n- 💚 Mint Green\n- 🧡 Sunset\n- ❤️ Cherry Red\n\nEach one changes the entire site — background, headings, tags, buttons, borders, everything. Your chosen theme saves to localStorage so it remembers your pick next time you open the page.\n\nRefresh and try clicking the color wheel button! Want me to add more themes or let you pick a custom color?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> i think the cursoring of mouse should change also align with the theme?",
    "\n\nGood call! Right now the sparkles are always blue regardless of theme. Let me make them match the active theme.\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "Now let me also check the floating hearts emojis:\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",

    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now hook `updateFloats` into the `applyTheme` function:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Now when you switch themes, everything updates together:\n\n- 🖱️ Cursor sparkles match the theme (pink hearts for Rose, purple crystals for Lavender, etc.)\n- 🫧 Floating background emojis change too (roses for Cherry Red, leaves for Mint Green, etc.)\n\nRefresh and try switching between themes — the whole vibe changes now! Want anything else?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> the heart in kirsty & Jet should also change color right?",
    "\n\nYou're right! The 💙 in the header is hardcoded. Let me make it change with the theme too.\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now add a heart emoji per theme and update it in `applyTheme`:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Also update the footer and page title hearts:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Now when you switch themes, the heart changes everywhere:\n\n- Header: \"Kirsty & Jet 💗\" / \"Kirsty & Jet 💜\" etc.\n- Footer: \"Made with 💚\" etc.\n- Browser tab title updates too\n\nEverything's fully synced with the theme now. Refresh and try it!\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> can you add also in our interests City Lights and Sponty events",

    "> can you add also a calender where we can fill our date or events in life",

    "> y",

    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! You now have an \"Our Calendar 📅\" section with:\n\n- ‹ › arrows to navigate months\n- Click any date to select it\n- Today's date is highlighted with an outline\n- Dates with events show a colored dot\n- Add events by selecting a date, typing in the input, and hitting \"Add 📅\" or Enter\n- 🗑️ button to remove events\n- Everything saves to localStorage\n\nRefresh and try clicking on a date then adding an event! Perfect for anniversaries, date nights, and sponty events 😄\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> in the add buttons emoji can we make it also align with the theme color",
    "\n\nThe \"Add 💙\" and \"Add 📅\" button emojis are hardcoded. Let me make them update with the theme.\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",

    "> can you make like glass the our interest, our bucket list and our calendar ",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! The sections now have a proper glassmorphism look:\n\n- More transparent background (`0.2` opacity instead of `0.65`)\n- Stronger blur (`16px`)\n- Subtle white border for that glass edge effect\n- Works on Safari too with `-webkit-backdrop-filter`\n\nRefresh and you'll see the gradient showing through the cards like frosted glass! Want me to tweak the transparency level?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> can you make our code structured because we will upload or host this in github so it can be live",
    "\n\nLet me split everything into separate files and set it up for GitHub Pages.\n[Tool uses: fs_read]",
    "Now I'll create the structured files:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now create the assets folder and a README:\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "All structured! Here's your project now:\n\n```\nlove-site/\n├── index.html          ← clean HTML only\n├── css/\n│   └── style.css       ← all styles\n├── js/\n│   └── app.js          ← all functionality\n├── assets/\n│   └── (music.mp3)     ← drop your music here\n└── README.md           ← setup & deploy guide\n```\n\nNote the music path changed to `assets/music.mp3`, so drop your audio file in the `assets/` folder now.\n\nTo deploy on GitHub Pages:\n1. Create a new repo on GitHub\n2. Run these in terminal:\n```bash\ncd ~/Downloads/love-site\ngit init\ngit add .\ngit commit -m \"our little corner of the internet 💙\"\ngit branch -M main\ngit remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_REPO.git\ngit push -u origin main\n```\n3. Go to repo Settings → Pages → Source → main branch\n4. Your site goes live at `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/YOUR_REPO/`\n\nWant me to help you initialize the git repo right now?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> yes",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "Git needs your identity first. What name and email do you want to use for the commits? (This should match your GitHub account.)\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> what can i name for this repo?",
    "\n\nHere are some ideas:\n\n- `kirsty-and-jet` — clean and simple\n- `our-little-corner` — matches the tagline on the site\n- `us-forever` — sweet and short\n- `love-page` — straightforward\n- `k-and-j` — minimal vibes\n\nIf you want it as your main GitHub Pages site, you could also name it `YOUR_USERNAME.github.io` and it'll be live at that URL directly.\n\nPick whichever feels right! Also, I still need your name and email for the git config — what should I use?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> https://github.com/Jerwin0210/our-little-corner.git",
    "\n\nI still need your name and email for the git commit. What should I set? Something like:\n\n- Name: `Jerwin0210`\n- Email: your GitHub email\n\nWhat email do you use on GitHub?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> jerwin.cuchapin@gmail.com",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "The commit is ready but GitHub needs authentication to push. You'll need to either:\n\n1. Use GitHub CLI (easiest):\n   ```bash\n   brew install gh\n   gh auth login\n   ```\n   Then run:\n   ```bash\n   cd ~/Downloads/love-site && git push -u origin main\n   ```\n\n2. Or use a Personal Access Token:\n   - Go to GitHub → Settings → Developer Settings → Personal Access Tokens → Generate new token\n   - Give it `repo` scope\n   - When prompted for password during push, paste the token instead\n\n3. Or set up SSH:\n   - Go to your repo page and switch the remote to SSH:\n   ```bash\n   git remote set-url origin git@github.com:Jerwin0210/our-little-corner.git\n   ```\n\nWhich method works best for you? Once pushed, go to repo Settings → Pages → Source → `main` branch and your site will be live at `https://jerwin0210.github.io/our-little-corner/` 💙\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> is possible to have a loading screen to make it aesthetic as well",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now add the loader styles:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now add the JS to dismiss the loader and update its heart with the theme:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Now when you open the site you get a loading screen with:\n\n- 💙 A pulsing heart emoji (matches the active theme color)\n- \"Kirsty & Jet\" in cursive that gently fades in and out\n- Gradient background matching the theme\n- Smoothly fades away after 1.5 seconds\n\nRefresh the page to see it! Want me to adjust the duration or add anything else to the loader?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> yes",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "Opened! You should see the pulsing heart loader first, then it fades into the main site. How does it look? Want any tweaks?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> can you make it when the loader finish its like theres an effect as well",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Now make the main content slide up into view:\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "\n[Tool uses: fs_write]",
    "> y",
    "Done! Now when the loader finishes:\n\n1. 💙 The heart bursts outward and fades (scales up to 4x)\n2. The loader zooms out slightly as it disappears\n3. The main content slides up smoothly into view\n\nRefresh and check it out! Want me to adjust the timing or effect?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> yes",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "There you go! Watch the heart burst and the content slide up. How's it looking? Want anything tweaked?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> yes",
    "\n[Tool uses: execute_bash]",
    "> y",
    "Opened! How does everything look? Is there anything you'd like to change or add?\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> save this convo",
    "\n\nYou can save this conversation by running the `/save` command. Just type:\n\n```\n/save\n```\n\nIt'll save the full conversation so you can pick up where we left off anytime with `/load`. 💙\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> /save",
    "> /chat save",
    "> /chat save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/",
    "> /chat save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/ --f",
    "> /chat save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/ --force",
    "> rror: unexpected argument '--f' found> \n> \n  tip: a similar argument exists: '--force'> \n> \nUsage: /chat save --force <PATH>> \n> \nFor more information, try '--help'.> \n> \nArguments:> \n  <PATH>  Path where the chat session will be saved> \n> \nOptions:> \n  -f, --force  Force overwrite if file already exists> \n  -h, --help   Print help> \n> \n> \n36% > /chat save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/ --force> \n> \n> \nFailed to export to /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/: Is a directory (os error 21)",
    "\n\nYou need to specify a file path, not a directory. Try:\n\n```\n/save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/chat-history.md\n```\n\nThat'll save the conversation as a file inside your project folder!\n[Tool uses: none]",
    "> /save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/chat-history.md",
    "> /chat save /Users/erwin.c/Downloads/love-site/chat-history.md"
  ],
  "tools": {
    "native___": [
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "use_subagent",
          "description": "⚠️ CRITICAL DELEGATION TOOL ⚠️\n\n🔍 BEFORE attempting ANY task, CHECK if you have the required tools in YOUR current tool list.\n\n❌ If you DON'T have the necessary tools → YOU MUST use this tool to delegate to a subagent that does.\n✅ If you DO have the tools → Handle the task yourself.\n\n## When to Use (MANDATORY scenarios):\n\n1. **MISSING TOOLS**: The user asks you to do something but you don't see the required tool in your available tools list\n   - Example: User asks to read a file, but you don't have 'fs_read' → USE THIS TOOL\n   - Example: User asks to search code, but you don't have 'code' tool → USE THIS TOOL\n   - Example: User asks to run bash command, but you don't have 'execute_bash' → USE THIS TOOL\n\n2. **PARALLEL PROCESSING**: A complex task can be split into independent subtasks that different specialized agents can handle simultaneously\n\n3. **CAPABILITY CHECK**: Use ListAgents command first to see what specialized agents and their toolsets are available\n\n## How Subagents Are Different:\n- Subagents have DIFFERENT, SPECIALIZED toolsets than you\n- Each subagent may have tools you don't have access to\n- They operate independently with their own context\n- Up to 4 subagents can work in parallel\n\n## Decision Flow:\n```\nUser makes request → Check YOUR tools list → Missing required tool? → USE use_subagent\n                                          → Have required tool? → Handle it yourself\n```\n\n⚡ Remember: Don't apologize about lacking tools - just delegate to a subagent that has them! Also note that subagents that are spawned together could not communicate with each other. If they are to perform tasks that are dependent on each other. Spawn them with a different tool call!",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "type": "object",
              "required": [
                "command"
              ],
              "properties": {
                "content": {
                  "properties": {
                    "subagents": {
                      "type": "array",
                      "items": {
                        "properties": {
                          "query": {
                            "description": "The query or task to be handled by the subagent",
                            "type": "string"
                          },
                          "agent_name": {
                            "type": "string",
                            "description": "Optional name of the specific agent to use. If not provided, uses the default agent"
                          },
                          "relevant_context": {
                            "description": "Optional additional context that should be provided to the subagent to help it understand the task better",
                            "type": "string"
                          }
                        },
                        "required": [
                          "query"
                        ],
                        "type": "object"
                      },
                      "description": "Array of subagent invocations to execute in parallel. Each invocation specifies a query, optional agent name, and optional context."
                    }
                  },
                  "required": [
                    "subagents"
                  ],
                  "type": "object",
                  "description": "Required for `InvokeSubagents` command. Contains subagents array and optional conversation ID."
                },
                "command": {
                  "description": "The commands to run. Allowed options are `ListAgents` to query available agents, or `InvokeSubagents` to invoke one or more subagents",
                  "type": "string",
                  "enum": [
                    "ListAgents",
                    "InvokeSubagents"
                  ]
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "fs_read",
          "description": "Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.\n\nFor single operation: provide array with one element.\nFor batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.\n\nAvailable modes:\n- Line: Read lines from a file\n- Directory: List directory contents\n- Search: Search for patterns in files\n- Image: Read and process images\n\nExamples:\n1. Single: {\"operations\": [{\"mode\": \"Line\", \"path\": \"/file.txt\"}]}\n2. Batch: {\"operations\": [{\"mode\": \"Line\", \"path\": \"/file1.txt\"}, {\"mode\": \"Search\", \"path\": \"/file2.txt\", \"pattern\": \"test\"}]}",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "properties": {
                "operations": {
                  "minItems": 1,
                  "description": "Array of operations to execute. Provide one element for single operation, multiple for batch.",
                  "items": {
                    "type": "object",
                    "properties": {
                      "mode": {
                        "type": "string",
                        "description": "The operation mode to run in: `Line`, `Directory`, `Search`. `Line` and `Search` are only for text files, and `Directory` is only for directories. `Image` is for image files, in this mode `image_paths` is required.",
                        "enum": [
                          "Line",
                          "Directory",
                          "Search",
                          "Image"
                        ]
                      },
                      "start_line": {
                        "type": "integer",
                        "default": 1,
                        "description": "Starting line number (optional, for Line mode). A negative index represents a line number starting from the end of the file."
                      },
                      "end_line": {
                        "type": "integer",
                        "description": "Ending line number (optional, for Line mode). A negative index represents a line number starting from the end of the file.",
                        "default": -1
                      },
                      "pattern": {
                        "description": "Pattern to search for (required, for Search mode). Case insensitive. The pattern matching is performed per line.",
                        "type": "string"
                      },
                      "offset": {
                        "default": 0,
                        "type": "integer",
                        "description": "Number of entries to skip for pagination (optional, for Directory mode). Use with max_entries to iterate through large directories. Entries are sorted by last modified time (most recent first). Default: 0"
                      },
                      "max_entries": {
                        "type": "integer",
                        "description": "Maximum number of entries to return (optional, for Directory mode). When limit is reached, results are truncated and metadata shows 'showing X of Y entries'. Use to prevent context window overflow. Default: 1000",
                        "default": 1000
                      },
                      "exclude_patterns": {
                        "items": {
                          "type": "string"
                        },
                        "default": [
                          "node_modules",
                          ".git",
                          "dist",
                          "build",
                          "out",
                          ".cache",
                          "target"
                        ],
                        "type": "array",
                        "description": "Glob patterns to exclude from directory listing (optional, for Directory mode). If omitted, uses defaults. If empty array [] is provided, no exclusions are applied (shows everything). If patterns are provided, they completely override the defaults. Examples: '**/target/**', '*.log'"
                      },
                      "path": {
                        "description": "Path to the file or directory. The path should be absolute, or otherwise start with ~ for the user's home (required for Line, Directory, Search modes).",
                        "type": "string"
                      },
                      "depth": {
                        "description": "Depth of a recursive directory listing (optional, for Directory mode)",
                        "type": "integer",
                        "default": 0
                      },
                      "context_lines": {
                        "default": 2,
                        "description": "Number of context lines around search results (optional, for Search mode)",
                        "type": "integer"
                      },
                      "image_paths": {
                        "type": "array",
                        "items": {
                          "type": "string"
                        },
                        "description": "List of paths to the images. This is currently supported by the Image mode."
                      }
                    },
                    "required": [
                      "mode"
                    ]
                  },
                  "type": "array"
                },
                "summary": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Optional description of the purpose of this batch operation (mainly useful for multiple operations)"
                }
              },
              "type": "object",
              "required": [
                "operations"
              ]
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "web_search",
          "description": "WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.\nTool performs basic compliance wrt content licensing and restriction.\nAs an agent you are responsible for adhering to compliance and attribution requirements.\nIMPORTANT: The snippets often contain enough information to answer questions - only use web_fetch if you need more detailed content from a specific webpage.\n\n## When to Use\n- When the user asks for current or up-to-date information (e.g., pricing, versions, technical specs) or explicitly requests a web search.\n- When verifying information that may have changed recently, or when the user provides a specific URL to inspect.\n\n## When NOT to Use\n- When the question involves basic concepts, historical facts, or well-established programming syntax/technical documentation.\n- When the topic does not require current or evolving information.\n\nFor any code-related tasks, follow this order:\n1. Search within the repository (if tools are available) and check if it can be inferred from existing code or documentation.\n2. Use this tool only if still unresolved and the library/data is likely new/unseen.\n\n## Content Compliance Requirements\nYou MUST adhere to strict licensing restrictions and attribution requirements when using search results:\n\n### Attribution Requirements\n- ALWAYS provide inline links to original sources using format: [description](url)\n- If not possible to provide inline link, add sources at the end of file\n- Ensure attribution is visible and accessible\n\n### Verbatim Reproduction Limits\n- NEVER reproduce more than 30 consecutive words from any single source\n- Track word count per source to ensure compliance\n- Always paraphrase and summarize rather than quote directly\n- Add compliance note when the content from the source is rephrased: \"Content was rephrased for compliance with licensing restrictions\"\n\n### Content Modification Guidelines\n- You MAY paraphrase, summarize, and reformat content\n- You MUST NOT materially change the underlying substance or meaning\n- Preserve factual accuracy while condensing information\n- Avoid altering core arguments, data, or conclusions\n\n## Usage Details\n- You may rephrase user queries to improve search effectiveness\n- You can make multiple queries to gather comprehensive information\n- Consider breaking complex questions into focused searches\n- Refine queries based on initial results if needed\n\n## Output Usage\n- Prioritize latest published sources based on publishedDate\n- Prefer official documentation to blogs and news posts\n- Use domain information to assess source authority and reliability\n\n## Error Handling\n- If unable to comply with content restrictions, explain limitations to user\n- Suggest alternative approaches when content cannot be reproduced\n- Prioritize compliance over completeness when conflicts arise\n\n## Output\nThe tool returns a JSON object with a \"results\" array containing search results:\n\n{\n  \"results\": [\n    {\n      \"title\": \"Example Page Title\",\n      \"url\": \"https://example.com/page\",\n      \"snippet\": \"Brief excerpt from the page...\",\n      \"publishedDate\": \"2025-11-20T10:30:00Z\",\n      \"domain\": \"example.com\",\n      \"id\": \"unique-id-123\",\n      \"maxVerbatimWordLimit\": 30,\n      \"publicDomain\": false\n    }\n  ]\n}\n\n## UI FROM LLM (You) back to the user\nCRITICAL: Always start your response with \"Here's what I found:\" and then start from a newline.\nALWAYS end your response with a blank line followed by 'References:' and list the sources you used in sequential order [1], [2], [3], etc. with NO gaps in numbering. Format: '[N] Title - URL' one per line. Truncate long titles to 80 characters and long URLs to 100 characters, adding '...' if truncated.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "required": [
                "query"
              ],
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "query": {
                  "description": "Search query - can be keywords, questions, or specific topics",
                  "type": "string"
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "fs_write",
          "description": "A tool for creating and editing files\n * The `create` command will override the file at `path` if it already exists as a file, and otherwise create a new file\n * The `append` command will add content to the end of an existing file, automatically adding a newline if the file doesn't end with one. The file must exist.\n Notes for using the `str_replace` command:\n * The `old_str` parameter should match EXACTLY one or more consecutive lines from the original file. Be mindful of whitespaces!\n * If the `old_str` parameter is not unique in the file, the replacement will not be performed. Make sure to include enough context in `old_str` to make it unique\n * The `new_str` parameter should contain the edited lines that should replace the `old_str`.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "required": [
                "command",
                "path"
              ],
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "path": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Absolute path to file or directory, e.g. `/repo/file.py` or `/repo`."
                },
                "old_str": {
                  "description": "Required parameter of `str_replace` command containing the string in `path` to replace.",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "command": {
                  "enum": [
                    "create",
                    "str_replace",
                    "insert",
                    "append"
                  ],
                  "description": "The commands to run. Allowed options are: `create`, `str_replace`, `insert`, `append`.",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "file_text": {
                  "description": "Required parameter of `create` command, with the content of the file to be created.",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "insert_line": {
                  "description": "Required parameter of `insert` command. The `new_str` will be inserted AFTER the line `insert_line` of `path`.",
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "new_str": {
                  "description": "Required parameter of `str_replace` command containing the new string. Required parameter of `insert` command containing the string to insert. Required parameter of `append` command containing the content to append to the file.",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "summary": {
                  "description": "A brief explanation of what the file change does or why it's being made.",
                  "type": "string"
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "execute_bash",
          "description": "Execute the specified bash command. NEVER prefix commands with cd to change the working directory, use the `working_dir` argument instead.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "properties": {
                "command": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Bash command to execute"
                },
                "summary": {
                  "description": "A brief explanation of what the command does",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "working_dir": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Working directory for command execution. Supports tilde expansion (e.g., ~/projects). If not specified, uses the current working directory."
                }
              },
              "type": "object",
              "required": [
                "command"
              ]
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "use_aws",
          "description": "Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "required": [
                "region",
                "service_name",
                "operation_name",
                "label"
              ],
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "service_name": {
                  "pattern": "^[^-].*",
                  "description": "The name of the AWS service. If you want to query s3, you should use s3api if possible. Must not start with a dash (-).",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "positional_args": {
                  "items": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "type": "array",
                  "description": "Positional arguments for high-level commands (e.g., s3 cp, s3 mv, s3 sync, s3 rm). These are passed directly without -- prefix. Use this for source/destination paths in S3 commands."
                },
                "region": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Region name for calling the operation on AWS."
                },
                "parameters": {
                  "description": "The parameters for the operation. The parameter keys MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. You should prefer to use JSON Syntax over shorthand syntax wherever possible. For parameters that are booleans, prioritize using flags with no value. Denote these flags with flag names as key and an empty string as their value. You should also prefer kebab case.",
                  "type": "object"
                },
                "operation_name": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "The name of the operation to perform."
                },
                "profile_name": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Optional: AWS profile name to use from ~/.aws/credentials. Defaults to default profile if not specified."
                },
                "label": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Human readable description of the api that is being called."
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "report_issue",
          "description": "Opens the browser to a pre-filled gh (GitHub) issue template to report chat issues, bugs, or feature requests. Pre-filled information includes the conversation transcript, chat context, and chat request IDs from the service.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "properties": {
                "title": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "The title of the GitHub issue."
                },
                "steps_to_reproduce": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Optional: Previous user chat requests or steps that were taken that may have resulted in the issue or error response."
                },
                "actual_behavior": {
                  "description": "Optional: The actual chat behavior that happened and demonstrates the issue or lack of a feature.",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "expected_behavior": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Optional: The expected chat behavior or action that did not happen."
                }
              },
              "required": [
                "title"
              ],
              "type": "object"
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "code",
          "description": "Code intelligence to analyze code with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.\n\nIMPORTANT: Prefer this tool over fs_read for code files. This tool intelligently provides structured code analysis.\n\nCORE FEATURES:\n• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)\n• Extracts function/class signatures via AST\n• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)\n• Codebase overview and directory exploration\n\nNOTE: Use fs_read with line ranges for unsupported patterns.\n\n## Operations\n\n**search_symbols** - Find symbol definitions by name across workspace.\nParams: symbol_name (required), path, symbol_type, limit, language, exact_match\nUses fuzzy matching by default. Set exact_match=true for precise matching.\n\n**lookup_symbols** - Batch lookup specific symbols (max 10).\nParams: symbols (required), file_path, include_source\nSet include_source=false for large classes/structs to avoid context overflow.\nScoped lookup: Provide file_path to search within specific file only.\n\n**get_document_symbols** - List all symbols in a file.\nParams: file_path (required), top_level_only (recommended: true)\nUse this for understanding file structure without reading content.\nPrefer this over fs_read when you need symbol information.\n\n**pattern_search** - AST-based structural search using ast-grep.\nParams: pattern, language (required), file_path, limit\n\n**pattern_rewrite** - AST-based code transformation.\nParams: pattern, replacement, language (required), file_path, limit, dry_run\nUse dry_run=true first to preview changes.\n\n## Pattern Search & Rewrite\n\nMetavariables:\n• $VAR - Matches single node (identifier, expression)\n• $$$ - Matches zero or more nodes (statements, parameters)\n\nSimple patterns:\n• console.log($ARG) - Match any console.log call\n• function $NAME($$$PARAMS) {} - Match function declaration\n• $OBJ.$METHOD() - Match any method call\n\nStructural rules (YAML):\n• pattern - Match code structure\n• kind - Match AST node type (function_declaration, class_declaration)\n• has - Node contains descendant matching pattern\n• inside - Node is inside ancestor matching pattern\n• all/any/not - Logical operators\n\nCritical: For relational rules (has, inside), always use stopBy: end\n\nRewrite examples:\n• pattern='var $N = $V', replacement='const $N = $V'\n• pattern='$O.hasOwnProperty($P)', replacement='Object.hasOwn($O, $P)'\n\nWorkflow:\n1. Use pattern_search first to verify matches\n2. Review matches to ensure correctness\n3. Test rewrite on small subset\n4. Apply to full codebase after verification\n\n**generate_codebase_overview** - ONLY for explicit architecture questions. Not for bug fixes or finding code.\nParams: path (optional)\n\n**search_codebase_map** - Focused directory exploration.\nParams: file_path, path\n\n## Explore Unfamiliar Code\n1. search_symbols for domain-specific types/functions/methods/classes\n2. lookup_symbols with include_source=true on promising matches\n3. grep (max 2 searches) for literal text patterns\n4. Repeat 1-3 with refined terms if needed\n5. generate_codebase_overview ONLY for architecture review or full codebase understanding\n   - NOT for bug fixes, feature work, or finding specific code\n\n## Tool Selection Priority\n1. search_symbols or lookup_symbols for finding code - ALWAYS use for functions/methods/classes/structs/interfaces\n2. get_document_symbols for file structure\n3. grep - ONLY for literal text in comments/strings, config values, code\n4. fs_read - raw file content when needed\n\n## CRITICAL RULES\n- Start with search_symbols\n- Always follow search_symbols with lookup_symbols\n- When searching for multiple related symbols, call search_symbols in parallel\n\n## Quick Reference\n• \"What's in this file?\" → get_document_symbols\n• \"Show me X class\" → search_symbols, then lookup_symbol\n\n## LSP Operations (Not Available)\nThese require LSP initialization (/code init):\nfind_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "symbol_name": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Simple symbol name, not qualified (e.g. 'myFunction' not 'MyClass.myFunction'). Required for search_symbols"
                },
                "path": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Directory path (optional, for generate_codebase_overview: workspace root, for search_codebase_map: path filter, for search_symbols: scope search to file or directory)"
                },
                "include_source": {
                  "type": "boolean",
                  "description": "Include source code in results (optional for lookup_symbols)"
                },
                "pattern": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "AST pattern (required for pattern_search)"
                },
                "limit": {
                  "description": "Maximum results (optional for search_symbols/pattern_search)",
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "file_path": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "File path (required for get_document_symbols, optional for pattern_search/search_codebase_map)"
                },
                "language": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Programming language (required for pattern_search, optional for search_symbols)"
                },
                "operation": {
                  "description": "The code intelligence operation to perform",
                  "type": "string",
                  "enum": [
                    "search_symbols",
                    "lookup_symbols",
                    "get_document_symbols",
                    "pattern_search",
                    "generate_codebase_overview",
                    "search_codebase_map",
                    "pattern_rewrite"
                  ]
                },
                "symbols": {
                  "items": {
                    "type": "string"
                  },
                  "type": "array",
                  "description": "List of simple symbol names, not qualified (e.g. ['myFunction'] not ['MyClass.myFunction']). Required for lookup_symbols, max 10"
                }
              },
              "required": [
                "operation"
              ]
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "glob",
          "description": "Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore. Prefer this over the bash 'find' command for listing or discovering paths. Returns JSON with totalFiles (count found), truncated (true if limited), and filePaths array. When truncated is true, just mention results are truncated, don't state the limit number.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "properties": {
                "max_depth": {
                  "description": "Maximum directory depth to traverse. Increase for deep nested structures.",
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "pattern": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Glob pattern, e.g. \"**/*.rs\", \"src/**/*.{ts,tsx}\" or \"**/test*\"."
                },
                "path": {
                  "description": "Root directory to search from. Only set this when the user explicitly mentions a directory path. In all other cases, omit this so the tool searches from the current working directory (the project root).",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "limit": {
                  "type": "integer",
                  "description": "Maximum files to return. If totalFiles exceeds this, truncated will be true."
                }
              },
              "type": "object",
              "required": [
                "pattern"
              ]
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "web_fetch",
          "description": "Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content). Use 'selective' mode to read specific parts of a page multiple times without filling context. Provide 'search_terms' in selective mode to find relevant sections (e.g., 'pricing', 'installation').",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "search_terms": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Optional: Keywords to find in selective mode (e.g., 'pricing cost', 'installation setup'). Returns ~10 lines before and after matches. If not provided, returns beginning of page."
                },
                "url": {
                  "description": "URL to fetch content from",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "mode": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "enum": [
                    "selective",
                    "truncated",
                    "full"
                  ],
                  "description": "Extraction mode: 'selective' for smart extraction (default), 'truncated' for first 8000 chars, 'full' for complete content"
                }
              },
              "required": [
                "url"
              ]
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "session",
          "description": "Adjust session settings temporarily (in-memory only, cleared on exit). CRITICAL: You MUST use introspect tool FIRST to verify setting names and understand their purpose before using set operation.\n\n## Session vs Persistent Settings\n- **session tool**: Temporary in-memory changes (cleared when chat exits) - Use for quick experiments or one-time adjustments\n- **fs_write tool**: Permanent changes saved to disk - Use when user says \"save\", \"persist\", \"permanently\", or \"always\"\n  - Global settings: ~/.kiro/settings.json\n  - Workspace settings: .kiro/settings.json\n\n## When to Use Each Tool\n- User: \"disable markdown\" → session tool (temporary)\n- User: \"disable markdown permanently\" → fs_write to ~/.kiro/settings.json (global) or .kiro/settings.json (workspace)\n- User: \"save this setting\" → fs_write (persistent)\n- User: \"try disabling markdown\" → session tool (temporary)\n\n## REQUIRED Workflow for Setting Changes\n1. User asks to change a setting\n2. Use introspect tool to find the correct setting name and understand what it does\n3. Determine if temporary (session) or permanent (fs_write)\n4. Use appropriate tool\n\n## Operations\n- **list**: Show currently configured session settings (non-default values only)\n- **get**: Get the current value of a specific setting\n- **set**: Change a setting value temporarily (MUST verify setting name with introspect first)\n- **reset**: Clear session override for a specific setting, or all session overrides if no key provided\n\n## Example Workflows\nTemporary change:\n  User: \"disable markdown\"\n  1. introspect(query=\"markdown setting\") → learn about chat.disableMarkdownRendering\n  2. session(operation=\"set\", key=\"chat.disableMarkdownRendering\", value=true)\n\nReset single setting:\n  User: \"reset markdown setting\"\n  session(operation=\"reset\", key=\"chat.disableMarkdownRendering\")\n\nReset all session overrides:\n  User: \"clear all my temporary settings\"\n  session(operation=\"reset\")\n\nPermanent change:\n  User: \"disable markdown permanently\"\n  1. introspect(query=\"markdown setting\") → learn about chat.disableMarkdownRendering\n  2. fs_write to modify ~/.kiro/settings.json with {\"chat.disableMarkdownRendering\": true}\n\nDO NOT guess setting names - always verify with introspect first.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "properties": {
                "key": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Setting key (e.g., 'chat.disableMarkdownRendering'). Required for 'get' and 'set' operations. Optional for 'reset' (if omitted, resets all session overrides). MUST be verified with introspect tool first."
                },
                "value": {
                  "description": "Value to set. Type depends on the setting (boolean, string, or number). Required for 'set' operation"
                },
                "operation": {
                  "enum": [
                    "list",
                    "get",
                    "set",
                    "reset"
                  ],
                  "description": "The operation to perform: 'list' shows configured settings, 'get' retrieves a specific setting, 'set' changes a setting value temporarily, 'reset' clears session override(s)",
                  "type": "string"
                }
              },
              "required": [
                "operation"
              ],
              "type": "object"
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "dummy",
          "description": "This is a dummy tool. If you are seeing this that means the tool associated with this tool call is not in the list of available tools. This could be because a wrong tool name was supplied or the list of tools has changed since the conversation has started. Do not show this when user asks you to list tools.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {},
              "required": []
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "introspect",
          "description": "Use ONLY when the user is asking about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities. Do NOT use for general coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform. When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/save', '/load', '/context'). CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details about any tool, feature, or command are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "properties": {
                "query": {
                  "description": "The user's question about this assistant's usage, features, or capabilities",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "doc_path": {
                  "description": "Path to a specific doc to retrieve (e.g., \"features/tangent-mode.md\"). Use this to get full content of a doc from the index.",
                  "type": "string"
                }
              },
              "type": "object",
              "required": []
            }
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "ToolSpecification": {
          "name": "grep",
          "description": "Fast text pattern search in files using regex. ALWAYS use this tool instead of 'grep', 'rg', or 'ag' commands in bash. Respects .gitignore.\n\n## Text Discovery Only\nUse grep for literal text/pattern matching: error messages, TODOs, config values, regex patterns.\n\n## For Semantic Code Understanding → Use 'code' tool if available\n- Finding symbol definitions or usages → code tool (search_symbols, goto_definition, find_references)\n- Understanding code structure/relationships → code tool\n- Distinguishing definition vs call vs import → code tool\n\n## Fallback\nIf the 'code' tool is available but returns insufficient symbol info, use grep to discover candidate files/lines, then return to 'code' for precise navigation.\n\nWhen you use this tool, prefer to show the user a small list of representative matches (including file paths and line numbers) instead of only giving a high-level summary.",
          "input_schema": {
            "json": {
              "type": "object",
              "required": [
                "pattern"
              ],
              "properties": {
                "pattern": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Regex pattern to search for. Examples: \"fn main\", \"class.*Component\", \"TODO|FIXME\". Start with simple patterns first (e.g. just the word you're looking for), then refine if needed."
                },
                "max_total_lines": {
                  "description": "Max total matched lines returned across all files (output limit). Increase when searching for many occurrences.",
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "output_mode": {
                  "description": "Output format: 'content' returns matches as 'file:line:content' (default, best for seeing actual matches), 'files_with_matches' returns only file paths, 'count' returns match counts per file.",
                  "type": "string",
                  "enum": [
                    "content",
                    "files_with_matches",
                    "count"
                  ]
                },
                "case_sensitive": {
                  "type": "boolean",
                  "description": "Case-sensitive search. Defaults to false (case-insensitive)."
                },
                "max_files": {
                  "description": "Max number of files returned (output limit). Increase for comprehensive codebase searches.",
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "max_matches_per_file": {
                  "type": "integer",
                  "description": "Max matches returned per file (output limit). Increase to see all occurrences in a file."
                },
                "path": {
                  "type": "string",
                  "description": "Directory to search from. Defaults to current working directory."
                },
                "include": {
                  "description": "File filter glob. Examples: \"*.rs\", \"*.{ts,tsx}\", \"*.py\"",
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "max_depth": {
                  "type": "integer",
                  "description": "Max directory depth to traverse when searching (search limit). Increase for deeply nested structures."
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },
